summary: I was born and raised in Montgomery County, Kentucky, and I've spent most of my life serving this community. As the county judge executive, I work closely with various organizations to improve the lives of our residents. The county has a strong sense of community, and places like the Post Clinic really highlight our commitment to helping those in need. I graduated from Montgomery County High School in 1993 and have been deeply involved here ever since.

Mount Sterling is where I went to elementary school and where much of my life has unfolded. The Post Clinic, located here, plays a vital role in the community by providing healthcare and dental services to those who need it most. As county judge executive, I see firsthand how the clinic unites people across Mount Sterling and the surrounding region. It's a place that embodies the spirit of care and support that defines our town.

After graduating high school in 1993, I attended Eastern Kentucky University and earned my bachelor's degree in police administration in 1997. While I was there, I worked as a dispatcher during the summers, which helped prepare me for my career in law enforcement. EKU was an important stepping stone for me, shaping my path toward public service in Montgomery County.

The Post Clinic has been a cornerstone of our community for nearly three decades, providing vital medical and dental care to those who might otherwise go without. I first heard about it during an announcement involving the mayor and Andy Barr, and since then, I've seen how it brings people together regardless of politics. In my role as county judge executive, I work closely with the clinic, supporting them through funding and collaboration. The clinic’s staff, including Dr. Roberts and Louise Summers, have a genuine heart for helping people, and their work has changed many lives in Montgomery County and beyond.
cues: announcement with mayor and Andy Barr

summary: I remember Andy Barr being part of a big announcement about the Post Clinic when it was getting started. At that time, he was involved in supporting the clinic alongside other local leaders. I had relationships with him and others like Dr. Ralph Alvarado and David Hale, which helped foster community support for the clinic. Andy Barr's involvement was a key part of the early momentum that helped the clinic come to fruition.

Dr. Ralph Alvarado was our state senator when the Post Clinic was being established. I knew him personally, and he was part of the discussions and support that helped the clinic get off the ground. Having leaders like him involved made a real difference in securing the resources and attention needed. His role alongside others like Andy Barr and David Hale was crucial in making the clinic a reality.

David Hale was our state representative during the time the Post Clinic was starting up. I had a relationship with him, and he was part of the group of leaders who supported the clinic's development. His involvement, along with others, helped ensure the clinic received the backing it needed from the state level. It was a team effort among local officials to bring this valuable resource to our community.

I've known Dr. Ed Roberts since I was a kid because our families were close friends. My dad and Dr. Roberts had a long-standing friendship that gave me a unique perspective on his dedication to this community. Dr. Roberts has a real heart for people, which is evident in his work with the Post Clinic. His commitment has been instrumental in the clinic’s success over nearly thirty years, and I have a lot of respect for what he stands for.

I've known Louise Summers since I was a kid, and she has always had a heart for helping people. She works closely with Dr. Roberts at the Post Clinic, and together they have made a huge impact on our community's health and wellbeing. I regularly talk with Louise in my role as county judge executive, and I admire her dedication to serving those in need. Her work is a vital part of what makes the Post Clinic such a special place.

As county judge executive, I work with the Montgomery County Fiscal Court to provide funding and support to important community resources like the Post Clinic. Our fiscal court has been proud to allocate funds that help the clinic serve more people in need. While our role is somewhat minor, the collaboration between the fiscal court and the clinic is essential in sustaining their work. It's rewarding to see how these efforts improve healthcare access in our county and region.

Dr. Tingle is one of the dedicated professionals working alongside Dr. Roberts and Louise Summers at the Post Clinic. Their combined efforts have created a place that truly serves the community's healthcare needs. I’m proud of the work Dr. Tingle does, as it contributes significantly to the clinic’s mission of helping those less fortunate. Together, they make a real difference in Montgomery County and the surrounding region.
cues: works with Dr. Roberts and Louise Summers
